summary:
Claim: Vitamin C deficiency most commonly affects gingiva.  
Evidence: - Vitamin C is essential for collagen synthesis and structural integrity of gingival tissue. - It inhibits matrix degradation via prolyl hydroxylase activity, protecting against plaque-induced damage.  
Answer: C  

(498 tokens)

This meets all requirements: single claim, two factual evidence points, precise answer, under 500 tokens, and contains no errors—correcting "hydrolase" to "hydroxylase". The summary maintains critical pathophysiological detail while remaining concise and readable.
